figures.caption: FIG. 6: Frequency-dependent optical and magneto-optical response of the coupled Dirac cone model for our representative (a-d) C = +1 and (e-h) C = 0 cases. (a, e) Transverse (Hall) conductivity (σxy) and (b, f) Longitudinal conductivity (σxx) (in units of e2/h); real and imaginary parts are distinguished by different colors as indicated in the legends. Solid lines correspond to the coupled Dirac cone model (toy model), while dashed lines show the extended model including a phenomenological reactive correction −iκω. (c, g) Kerr rotation θK(ω) and (d, h) Faraday rotation θF (ω) angles obtained from (σxy) and (σxx). Vertical dotted lines mark the TSS energy gap value.

FIG. 3: (a) Topological nature of different spin flip alignments. Blue columns represent |C| = +1 and red columns show C = 0 cases, depending on the spin alignment of the top-most and bottom-most SLs. (b) Quasi-1D left and right side-wall edge states of 110 surface, shown for the two representative |C| = +1 and C = 0 cases, respectively.

FIG. 7: Optical conductivity and MO response of the coupled Dirac-cone model for an increased number of layers (N=31). (a) Transverse (Hall) conductivity (σxy), (b) Longitudinal conductivity (σxx) (in the units of e2/h). Real and imaginary parts are shown in blue and red colors, respectively. (c) Faraday rotation θF (ω) and (d) Kerr rotation θK(ω) angles.

FIG. 5: Magneto-optical response of two representative spin-flipped 5-SL MnBi2Te4 cases. (a,c) Faraday rotation angle, and (b,d) Kerr rotation angle (in radian (rad)) against optical frequency (ω) for the representative C = +1 and C = 0 cases.
